target.name: Slip Robotics

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $44M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median

private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

data.narrative:
Slip Robotics is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company seeking valuation following $44M in total capital raised. The peer universe comprises 9 private companies and 1 public comparable, spanning Robotics & Manufacturing at Series B and Series C stages. Primary comparables include Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B), Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C), and Nimble Robotics ($1.0B, Series C).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $44M raised proxy, implying a valuation of $84M. Peer-median methodology across the four direct comparables yields a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $1.8B. The peer set reflects companies at equivalent or slightly advanced funding stages within the same sector.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $71M to $1.9B, with a median of $854M. This wide range reflects material methodological divergence: stage-progression yields $84M based on capital deployed, while peer comparables cluster near $1.6B. Key uncertainties include growth trajectory relative to peers, unit economics specific to Slip's applications, and market timing dynamics in private robotics valuations. Further diligence on revenue, customer concentration, and technical differentiation would narrow the range materially.
